_doc: Eval cases mined from MAIN history only (stable long-term). Each case = a real conversion the maintainers shipped to main. `input` is the pre-conversion (nonstrict) blob the worker starts from; `gold` is always "main" — the file as it lives on main HEAD now, which by construction matches the main-built sourcemap (so single-file analyze can never go stale). The harness lays `input` at the file's real path, runs the worker to convert it, then scores mechanically: header is --!strict, single-file analyze is clean, and `any` count vs the gold. Negatives come from the revert commit: a naive flip that broke downstream and was reverted, so on main the correct end-state is nonstrict.

cases.archetype: plain data/constants module — header flip, no class block
util module — type the function signatures (pure typing conversion)
inherited UI class — export type block + & Parent (Snackbar < TransitionModel)
stateful class with methods — dot-syntax self + export type
module importing t — `local t: any = require("t")` boundary
[settings pkg] util module with typed signatures
[settings pkg] core class — export type block
[settings pkg] base class others inherit from
[settings pkg] headline class — inherits PlayerSettingsBase (& Parent)
naive flip broke downstream; maintainer reverted — correct end-state is nonstrict
cases._note: input is the naive --!strict flip (pre-revert); gold (main) is the reverted --!nonstrict. A worker that blindly flips this must be caught by the downstream lint:luau gate.
